mission.title: Install and Configure Bootc on Kubernetes
mission.description: Bootc is a tool designed for transactional, in-place operating system updates using OCI/Docker container images. Installing Bootc allows you to manage and upgrade your operating system seamlessly using container technology.

steps.title: Add the Bootc Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official Bootc Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.
```bash
helm repo add bootc https://bootc-dev.github.io/bootc/charts
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Bootc using Helm
steps.description:
Install Bootc in the 'bootc' namespace, creating it if it doesn't exist, and specify the version to install.
```bash
helm install bootc bootc/bootc --version v1.13.0 --namespace bootc --create-namespace
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the Bootc pods are running successfully in the 'bootc' namespace.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace bootc
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
Update the Bootc deployment to set resource limits for better resource management.
```bash
kubectl set resources deployment bootc --limits=cpu=500m,memory=512Mi --namespace bootc
```

steps.title: Set up port forwarding for access
steps.description:
Forward a local port to the Bootc service to access it from your local machine.
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/bootc 8080:80 --namespace bootc
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Bootc will show the Bootc pods running in the 'bootc' namespace, and you will be able to access the service through port 8080 on your local machine. Resource limits will be applied to the Bootc deployment.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add bootc https://bootc-dev.github.io/bootc/charts
helm repo update
helm install bootc bootc/bootc --version v1.13.0 --namespace bootc --create-namespace
kubectl set resources deployment bootc --limits=cpu=500m,memory=512Mi --namespace bootc

prerequisites.description: You need a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.24 or higher, along with Helm and Kubectl installed on your local machine.
